run.true_intent: Whenever we publish a new product launch page, I want a short summary posted to our team's Notion 'Launches' database so everyone can see it. The trigger is a new launch being published; the destination is Notion, not chat.
run.vague_intent: post updates about our product launches so the team stays in the loop

transcript.question: Where should launch updates be posted?
transcript.answer: Notion — post it to our team's 'Launches' database, not a chat app.

transcript.question: What should trigger a launch update to be posted?
options: A new GitHub release is published
A Linear issue is marked as launched
A ClickUp task reaches a specific status
transcript.answer: A new launch page being published on our site — none of those. Manual is the closest, but really it should fire automatically when the launch goes live.

transcript.question: Should the agent post updates automatically, or let you review and approve each update before it goes out?
transcript.answer: Post automatically — nobody should have to babysit it, the summary just goes straight into the Notion database.

subscriptions.description: Inbound webhook fired by the site CMS/publishing pipeline when a launch page transitions to published state.

trigger.description: Fires automatically when a new launch page goes live on the product site. The event payload carries the raw launch details that seed the draft.

subscriptions.description: Receives the finalized launch update object produced by the draft capability once it is marked ready to publish.

trigger.description: Fires automatically when a launch_draft_accepted event is received, carrying the finalized update payload. No human approval step intervenes — the post goes out immediately upon draft acceptance.

run.true_intent: Exactly that: a weekday 9am standup that lists yesterday's merged PRs from our main repo and posts the summary to our #standup Slack channel. The only thing I didn't say is WHICH repo and WHICH Slack channel.
run.vague_intent: Every weekday at 9am, collect yesterday's merged pull requests from our repo and post a standup summary to Slack.

transcript.question: Which GitHub repository (or repositories) should the bot pull merged PRs from? Please provide the full repo path(s), e.g. `myorg/myrepo`.
transcript.answer: It's `acme-corp/platform` — that's our main repo, just the one.

transcript.question: Which Slack channel should the standup digest be posted to? Note: you have a Discord connector in your vault but no Slack connector — do you want to post to Discord instead, or will you add a Slack connector?
options: I'll add a Slack connector — post to Slack
transcript.answer: I'll add a Slack connector — post to Slack, channel #standup.

trigger.description: Fires every weekday at 08:00 — early enough for the digest to be ready before standup. Timezone should be updated to match the team's local timezone.

subscriptions.description: Fires when the PR-collection capability has finished building the digest. This capability consumes the emitted digest payload and proceeds to post it.

trigger.description: Weekdays at 08:45 local time so the digest lands in #standup before the 09:00 meeting.

run.true_intent: Honestly I just have one concrete pain: every morning I copy yesterday's Stripe payouts into a Google Sheet by hand. I want that one thing automated — pull yesterday's Stripe payouts and append them to my 'Revenue' sheet each morning. Nothing else.
run.vague_intent: manage my whole workflow and make my life easier
